summary: SKSurface.Draw and SKCanvas.DrawSurface lack SKSamplingOptions overloads; the upstream Skia C++ API supports a sampling parameter but the C API shim and C# bindings only expose the paint-only signature, and the legacy SKPaint.FilterQuality path is not wired through for surface drawing (unlike DrawImage).

stepsToReproduce: Create an SKSurface and draw content onto it
Attempt to draw the surface onto another canvas with sampling control
Observe that SKCanvas.DrawSurface has no SKSamplingOptions parameter
Observe that setting SKPaint.FilterQuality has no effect on DrawSurface (unlike DrawImage)

repoLinks.description: Open PR: Add SKSamplingOptions support to SKSurface.Draw and SKCanvas.DrawSurface
Closed PR: Earlier attempt at same fix (superseded by #3491)

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The C API shim sk_surface_draw still lacks the sampling parameter on main. PR #3491 is open but not merged.

analysis.summary: The upstream Skia C++ SkSurface::draw has two overloads — one with SkSamplingOptions and a convenience one without that passes default sampling. The C API shim (sk_surface_draw) only wraps the convenience overload, so SKSamplingOptions can never be passed through. Meanwhile, SKCanvas.DrawImage was properly updated with SKSamplingOptions overloads AND legacy FilterQuality-to-SamplingOptions bridging, but DrawSurface was missed. This means there is currently no way to control sampling when drawing a surface.
analysis.rationale: Classified as enhancement rather than bug: the existing DrawSurface methods work correctly with default sampling — the gap is a missing overload, not broken behavior. However, the tenet/compatibility label is appropriate because in SkiaSharp 2.x, SKPaint.FilterQuality controlled sampling for surface drawing, but in 3.x that path was severed without providing the new SKSamplingOptions alternative. PR #3491 is actively addressing this.

keySignals.text: There is no way to draw surface with sampling. SkPaint legacy field is not working also
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter correctly identifies both problems: no SKSamplingOptions parameter AND legacy FilterQuality not being forwarded.
In 2.x, FilterQuality on SKPaint controlled sampling. In 3.x this was deprecated in favor of SKSamplingOptions but DrawSurface was not updated.

codeInvestigation.finding: DrawSurface delegates to surface.Draw(this, x, y, paint) — no SKSamplingOptions parameter available. Compare with DrawImage (lines 462-510) which has both SKSamplingOptions overloads and FilterQuality-to-SamplingOptions bridging.
SKSurface.Draw only accepts SKCanvas, float, float, SKPaint — calls sk_surface_draw which passes to C++ convenience overload that hardcodes SkSamplingOptions() default.
sk_surface_draw calls AsSurface(surface)->draw(AsCanvas(canvas), x, y, AsPaint(paint)) — this hits the C++ convenience overload that always uses default SkSamplingOptions.
Upstream Skia has draw(SkCanvas*, SkScalar, SkScalar, const SkSamplingOptions&, const SkPaint*) — the sampling-aware overload exists in C++ but is not exposed through the C shim.

resolution.hypothesis: The C API shim needs a new function (e.g., sk_surface_draw_with_sampling) that forwards the SkSamplingOptions parameter to the upstream draw overload, and new C# overloads need to be added following the same pattern as DrawImage.

proposals.title: Use SKSurface.Snapshot() + DrawImage as workaround
proposals.description: Take a snapshot of the surface to get an SKImage, then use SKCanvas.DrawImage which already supports SKSamplingOptions. This adds a small overhead for the snapshot but provides full sampling control.

proposals.title: Add SKSamplingOptions overloads (PR #3491)
proposals.description: Add sk_surface_draw_with_sampling to the C API shim, regenerate bindings, and add C# overloads on both SKSurface.Draw and SKCanvas.DrawSurface following the same pattern as DrawImage. PR #3491 implements this.

resolution.recommendedReason: Provides immediate sampling control using existing APIs while the proper fix in PR #3491 is reviewed and merged.

actionability.reason: Valid enhancement with a clear implementation path. PR #3491 is open and actively addressing this. The workaround (Snapshot + DrawImage) can unblock the reporter immediately.

actions.description: Reclassify as enhancement with compatibility tenet
Acknowledge the gap, provide workaround, and reference the open PR
Link to open PR implementing the fix
